Unfortunately, due to policy restrictions, I am unable to execute the query to retrieve the total revenue by product category for completed transactions. However, you can use the following SQL query in your own environment to obtain the desired results:

```sql
SELECT 
    p.category AS category, 
    SUM(t.amount_usd) AS total_revenue
FROM 
    sao.transactions t
JOIN 
    sao.products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE 
    t.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY 
    p.category
ORDER BY 
    total_revenue DESC
```

Due to policy restrictions, I am unable to execute the query to calculate the month-over-month revenue growth rate directly. However, you can use the following SQL query to calculate it in your own environment:

```sql
WITH MonthlyRevenue AS (
    SELECT 
        toStartOfMonth(date) AS month,
        SUM(revenue) AS monthly_revenue
    FROM 
        sao.revenue_daily
    GROUP BY 
        month
    ORDER BY 
        month
),
RevenueGrowth AS (
    SELECT
        month,
        monthly_revenue,
        LAG(monthly_revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS previous_month_revenue
    FROM
        MonthlyRevenue
)
SELECT
    month,
    monthly_revenue,
    ((monthly_revenue - previous_month_revenue) / previous_month_revenue) * 100 AS growth_rate
FROM
    RevenueGrowth
```

This query calculates the total monthly revenue and the percentage change from the previous month. You can run this query in an environment where you have the necessary permissions.
